cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking why Ms. Leibfried asked the author to recite a passage in front of the class. The options are A, B, C, or D. First, I'll need to go back to the text and find the relevant part. The story mentions that the author was an introverted student who felt shy and was the only nonwhite student. She found solace in reading and writing. Ms. Leibfried noticed her interest and offered her reading suggestions. Then, when it came to the big book report project, Ms. Leibfried suggested she do something "a little different" instead of the usual report. The author chose a passage from "The Bell Jar" to recite. Afterward, she received applause, which made her feel confident for the first time. Now, the question is why Ms. Leibfried suggested this. Let's look at the options: A. To test whether she had a good memory. B. To make her earn the respect of her classmates. C. To help deepen her understanding of "The Bell Jar." D. To guide her to build a good relationship with others. Looking at the text, the main reason Ms. Leibfried suggested the recitation seems to be to give the author a chance to overcome her shyness and gain confidence. The passage mentions that the author was introverted and shy, and that Ms. Leibfried helped her feel less shy. By allowing her to recite a passage, maybe the teacher was helping her build confidence and perhaps connect with her classmates. The result was that the author felt confident for the first time. Option D says "To guide her to build a good relationship with others." But the class applauded her, but the main point here is about the teacher's intention. The teacher's action was supportive, encouraging the author to step out of her comfort zone, which led to her confidence. However, none of the options directly say "to build confidence." But perhaps the closest is D,
